evidence: The candidate disappears after modeling AUTO_KFREE/AUTO_KVFREE. misc.h:16-21 defines them as __free(kfree)/__free(kvfree), so the compiler releases the resource at scope exit.

The source explicitly treats a missing or corrupt replace item as no active replacement when no target device exists; the same policy is present in Linux v6.8 (dev-replace.c:95-122).
check_ref_cleanup() is used as a boolean decision; zero means no immediate tree-block cleanup is needed (extent-tree.c:3657-3661).
A return value of 1 from the writepage helpers means the folio was already handled, so the wrapper intentionally returns success (extent_io.c:1889-1898).
A return value of 1 from the writepage helpers means the folio was already handled, so the wrapper intentionally returns success (extent_io.c:1889-1898).
The reported exit is dominated by a NULL/ERR_PTR acquisition result; no live resource exists on this path. Other already-held resources are cleaned by the shown exit cleanup.
The source explicitly converts helper errors into the safe COW fallback after the helper has freed the path (inode.c:7491-7497).
The explicit cleanup branch covers insertion failure or an existing item and intentionally returns success after releasing the reservation and record (qgroup.c:2183-2189).
The reported exit is dominated by a NULL/ERR_PTR acquisition result; no live resource exists on this path. Other already-held resources are cleaned by the shown exit cleanup.
A short bio_add_page() result triggers size rollback and returns 0 to report that no bytes were added (raid56.c:1194-1208).

The grabbed fs_root->reloc_root reference survives recovery failures that do not set BTRFS_FS_ERROR. Linux v6.8 QEMU fault injection confirms the defect, and Linux v7.1 retains the assignment and out_unset cleanup shape (relocation.c:5609-5639).

The grabbed fs_root->reloc_root reference survives recovery failures that do not set BTRFS_FS_ERROR. Linux v6.8 QEMU fault injection confirms the defect, and Linux v7.1 retains the assignment and out_unset cleanup shape (relocation.c:5609-5639).
A positive btrfs_search_slot_for_read() result means no matching extent; zero correctly reports that the extent is not unchanged (send.c:6198-6202).
btrfs_search_slot() returning 1 means the key was not found, so returning 0 means the backreference is absent (tree-log.c:1193-1206).

check_next_block_uptodate() unlocks and frees next before returning an error (extent-tree.c:5793-5799); the caller must not release it again.
The constructor releases the extent buffer and returns NULL on failure (extent_io.c:3141-3163); NULL is the documented pointer failure result.
